page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.small is an entry-level virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU from an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the lower performance tiers. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng yields a score of 1061.69, categorizing it in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show 79.26 GB/sec for cached small blocks and drop to 8.61 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks show constrained throughput, with Redis handling 1.22 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput is limited to 1.22 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and minimal resource footprint, the ecs.n4.small is positioned as a highly cost-efficient option for low-intensity workloads where continuous high performance is not required. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU resource, which limits its suitability for CPU-bound or memory-intensive tasks. It is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development or testing environments, and simple background tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.small is an entry-level, KVM-based virtual machine featuring a single shared vCPU from an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Performance benchmarks consistently place this instance in the lower percentiles, with a stress-ng CPU score of 1061.69 and a Passmark CPU score of 1277.65. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX and AVX512 instructions, though memory bandwidth remains constrained, measuring 8.61 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Due to its shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity, it offers a highly economical profile for non-critical, low-resource tasks. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight background processes, and software development or testing environments that do not require sustained compute power.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.small is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 1 shared Intel Xeon vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, designed for low-intensity workloads and budget-conscious environments.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.n4.small is an entry-level virtual server featuring 1 shared Intel Xeon vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-core virtual server designed for lightweight background processes, basic web hosting, and non-critical software development environments.
bullet_points: 1 shared Intel Xeon 8269CY vCPU at 2.5 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
Low-tier benchmark performance across CPU and memory tests
Suited for basic web serving, testing, and lightweight background tasks
